g.text: BLOOM FILTER: 'is x in the set?'
COUNT-MIN SKETCH: 'how many times did x appear?'

text.tspan: ALL THREE SHARE:
u2022 SUBLINEAR memory in number of items
u2022 controlled approximation error (you choose the trade-off)
u2022 fast O(k) updates and queries (k = number of hash functions)
u2022 mergeable across shards (you can compute partials and combine)
u2022 acceptable inaccuracy for the use case
Answers: SET MEMBERSHIP (yes/no, with false positives only)
Memory: ~10 bits per item for 1% FPR
USE WHEN:
u2022 'is this URL malicious?' (Chrome, Squid)
u2022 'have I seen this key before?' (LSM-tree negative lookups)
u2022 'is this username taken?' (pre-check before DB query)
u2022 cache penetration prevention
FALSE POS: small chance yes when actually no
FALSE NEG: never
Answers: CARDINALITY (count of unique values)
Memory: ~1.5 KB total u2192 1.04 / sqrt(m) error u2248 1% with m=8192
USE WHEN:
u2022 unique-visitors per minute/hour/day
u2022 unique users in A/B test buckets
u2022 distinct query terms in a search log
u2022 DISTINCT in column-store databases (BigQuery, Redshift, Snowflake)
ERROR: u00b11-2% typically; constant memory regardless of cardinality
MERGEABLE: HLLs can be combined (union of sets)
Answers: FREQUENCY ESTIMATE (approximate count per key)
Memory: w u00d7 d ints; ~few KB for good accuracy
USE WHEN:
u2022 heavy hitters (top-K most frequent keys)
u2022 streaming counters that can't fit exact dict in memory
u2022 abuse / DDoS detection (count requests per IP)
u2022 NLP frequency tables in fixed memory
OVERESTIMATES (collisions add); never underestimates
Min estimator across rows minimizes the overestimate
